fact_summary: Triage is a rapid risk sort, not a diagnosis; subtle chronic or complex symptoms may appear low acuity if vitals are normal.
fact_context: Stage: nurse triage; action: Classify acuity using ESI: immediate life-saving intervention, high risk/severe pain, then expected resources, with danger-zone vitals review.
topic_category: red flag missed; normal-test trap; incomplete history
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: ESI is a documented front-door workflow; its resource prediction step can under-prioritize problems that need diagnostic synthesis rather than many ED resources.

fact_summary: High-risk diagnoses may be missed at the first visit and only recognized after repeat ED return or deterioration.
fact_context: Stage: first clinician assessment; imaging/lab interpretation; discharge; action: Systematic review estimated diagnostic error harms and common high-harm conditions.
topic_category: red flag missed; repeated visits ignored; no follow-up ownership
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Useful as cross-setting failure evidence rather than a local workflow manual.

fact_summary: Patients may experience an 'occult wait' before triage if registration queues or digital check-in create hidden delays.
fact_context: Stage: arrival; registration; nurse triage; streaming; first clinician assessment; action: Initial assessment can include streaming, triage and rapid assessment/treatment to identify sickest patients and direct others to right service.
topic_category: poor handoff; red flag missed; no follow-up ownership
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Represents a national ED front-door flow rather than a diagnostic guideline.

fact_summary: Operational queues can hide clinical risk before the patient is visible to the triage system.
fact_context: Stage: registration; initial assessment; vitals; action: Register quickly, perform initial assessment by trained registered clinician, record minimum clinical fields and use early warning scores where relevant.
topic_category: arrival; registration; red flag missed; poor handoff
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Supports the black-zone concept of pre-triage waits.

fact_summary: Decision support needs upstream data completeness and downstream reassessment when diagnosis does not fit.
fact_context: Stage: arrival; intake; labs ordered; imaging ordered; lab interpretation; discharge/admission; action: Patient arrives by EMS/walk-in; triage/intake RN collects key data; provider orders labs/CXR; CURB-65 and clinical assessment guide admission vs outpatient antibiotics.
topic_category: documentation error; no one connected the dots; normal-test trap
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Real implementation workflow shows how data from different roles enters a decision point.

fact_summary: Self-service can speed flow but may miss nuance, language barriers or worsening while waiting.
fact_context: Stage: arrival; intake form; triage; referral; action: Patient self-checks in on kiosk/tablet, completes symptom triage, and can be prioritised, redirected or referred through service routing.
topic_category: digital access gap; incomplete history; red flag missed
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: A current example of digital triage embedded in urgent/emergency care.

fact_summary: UTC boundaries are porous; patients may need escalation, diagnostics or follow-up ownership outside the UTC.
fact_context: Stage: arrival; first clinician assessment; labs ordered; imaging ordered; referral; follow-up; action: UTCs accept booked and walk-in patients; provide basic diagnostics or protocols; share records/post-event messages and refer to ED/specialists when needed.
topic_category: wrong specialty; poor handoff; no follow-up ownership
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: UTC standard describes setting-level capabilities and data-sharing requirements.

fact_summary: The workflow depends on patients knowing when and how to report deterioration while waiting.
fact_context: Stage: arrival; vitals; first clinician assessment; discharge/referral; action: On arrival, patients may have observations by nurse, may wait by acuity not arrival order, and can be assessed/treated onsite or referred; GP is updated afterwards.
topic_category: red flag missed; patient self-advocacy required
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Patient-facing description of the real UTC arrival process.

fact_summary: Vitals create early signal but can be incomplete, delayed or not trended against prior visits.
fact_context: Stage: vitals; nurse triage; first clinician assessment; action: Initial vitals tailored to age, complaint and status; abnormal vitals reported and repeated.
topic_category: normal-test trap; lab trend missed; documentation error
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Practical urgent-care policy source, not a population study.

fact_summary: Non-clinical intake staff may be the first safety barrier.
fact_context: Stage: registration; intake form; nurse triage; action: Registration/intake staff ask reason for visit; red flags trigger immediate rooming and clinician involvement or ED transfer.
topic_category: red flag missed; incomplete history; poor handoff
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Shows how triage starts before formal clinician assessment.

fact_summary: Digital triage may fragment complex histories into a single main symptom.
fact_context: Stage: before visit; intake form; referral; action: Patient completes one symptom pathway; service gives advice such as self-care, pharmacist, GP, UTC, ED or callback.
topic_category: incomplete history; wrong specialty; no one connected the dots
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Patient-facing NHS 111 online flow.

fact_summary: The handoff is only as good as the service directory and referral visibility.
fact_context: Stage: before visit; intake form; referral; action: Uses structured triage and service directory to recommend ambulance/ED/UTC/out-of-hours GP/GP/pharmacist/self-care outcomes.
topic_category: wrong specialty; poor handoff; fragmented care
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Staff-facing service information documents integration pieces.

fact_summary: Online access can create a digital bottleneck and reduce the chance to observe/examine patients.
fact_context: Stage: before visit; intake form; first clinician assessment; follow-up; action: Asynchronous online consultations support clinical/admin requests but may be capped or rerouted by practices for safety.
topic_category: digital access gap; incomplete history; no follow-up ownership; chronic illness invalidation
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: HSSIB source includes patient and staff focus groups and practice visits.

fact_summary: Telehealth changes the signal set available for diagnosis.
fact_context: Stage: medical history; physical exam; vitals; referral; follow-up; action: Telehealth can be safer when embedded in a medical home with records, tests, referrals and follow-up; risk rises when exam and communication are limited.
topic_category: normal-test trap; incomplete physical exam; poor follow-up ownership
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Useful cross-setting telehealth safety evidence.

fact_summary: Routing can optimize capacity but also become a diagnostic gate if symptoms are under-described.
fact_context: Stage: before visit; intake form; care navigation; first clinician assessment; action: Modern access model directs patients via phone/online/in-person to the right professional first time, using care navigation and demand/capacity management.
topic_category: incomplete history; wrong specialty; no one connected the dots
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: National primary-care access policy.

fact_summary: Ambiguity over whether a contact is administrative or clinical can lose risk signals.
fact_context: Stage: care navigation; clinical triage; referral; action: Care navigation signposts to appropriate clinician or service; clinical triage by nurse/GP makes early clinical decisions.
topic_category: poor handoff; symptom dismissed; red flag missed
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Professional guidance clarifies first-contact roles.

fact_summary: Threshold pathways may leave low-risk but persistent symptoms in a monitoring black zone.
fact_context: Stage: medical history; physical exam; labs ordered; referral; safety netting; action: Guideline organizes referral/investigation actions by cancer site, symptom and findings; includes safety netting and diagnostic-process advice.
topic_category: normal-test trap; referral delay; rare disease delay
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: A key formal workflow source for primary-care suspicion/referral.

fact_summary: A negative test can prematurely close a diagnostic episode.
fact_context: Stage: follow-up; repeat visit; diagnosis discussion; action: Safety-netting gives time-bound follow-up, documents advice, ensures tests/referrals completed and monitors persistent symptoms even after negative tests.
topic_category: normal-test trap; no follow-up ownership; repeated visits ignored
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Clear source for the normal-test trap and repeat-presentation workflows.

fact_summary: Test-result management crosses many people and organisations.
fact_context: Stage: labs ordered; lab interpretation; follow-up; action: Practice needs systems for ordering, tracking attendance, return/non-return, review, filing, patient communication and abnormal follow-up.
topic_category: abnormal result not followed up; documentation error; no follow-up ownership
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Regulator source supports result-management black zones.

fact_summary: No one clinician/system connected the repeated back-pain presentations with prior cancer risk.
fact_context: Stage: repeat visit; medical history; specialist review; emergency escalation; action: Over 8 months the patient saw many clinicians; eventual GP found spinal lump and sent patient to ED where CT found metastatic cancer.
topic_category: fragmented care; no one connected the dots; missing family/history context; repeated visits ignored
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Named public case from national safety investigation; includes patient voice.

fact_summary: Repeat visits are often visible only as isolated encounters.
fact_context: Stage: repeat visit; physical exam; referral; labs ordered; action: After three presentations, GP teams are prompted to reflect, review and rethink, consider face-to-face exam, second opinion, tests or referral.
topic_category: repeated visits ignored; no one connected the dots; bias or minimization
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Current NHS initiative; useful product pattern for persistent symptoms.

fact_summary: Chronic pain and women's health symptoms can be minimized despite repeated presentations.
fact_context: Stage: medical history; referral; specialist review; repeat visit; action: Survey documented repeated GP visits and A&E attendance before diagnosis; respondents reported dismissal/minimisation.
topic_category: symptom dismissed; chronic illness invalidation; bias or minimization; patient self-advocacy required
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Advocacy survey gives patient voice and quantitative delay data.

fact_summary: Referral is not a completed action unless the patient is seen, tests are done and results acted on.
fact_context: Stage: referral; follow-up; lab interpretation; action: Healthwatch gathered patient reports of waiting after GP referral, confusion over results and pressure to chase.
topic_category: referral delay; no follow-up ownership; patient self-advocacy required
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Patient voice source on referral/follow-up gaps.

fact_summary: Imaging workflows fail at multiple points: ordering, performance, interpretation, communication, follow-up.
fact_context: Stage: imaging ordered; lab interpretation; follow-up; emergency return; action: Ombudsman reported repeated failures in scan reading, carrying out scans and follow-up; a cancer case was only identified at a fifth A&E visit after lesion not reported.
topic_category: abnormal result not followed up; red flag missed; repeated visits ignored
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Patient-complaint/ombudsman evidence with public family quote.

fact_summary: Responsibility can diffuse between radiology, ED, GP and patient.
fact_context: Stage: imaging ordered; imaging interpretation; discharge; follow-up; action: Investigation traced multistep communication of unexpected significant findings and weak assurance that action occurred.
topic_category: poor handoff; abnormal result not followed up; no follow-up ownership
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Important source for test-result black zones beyond labs.

fact_summary: Discharge can sever ownership of pending results.
fact_context: Stage: discharge; follow-up; imaging/lab interpretation; action: Hospital diagnostic excellence framework encourages targeting pending tests and suspicious imaging follow-up.
topic_category: poor handoff; no follow-up ownership; abnormal result not followed up
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Current CDC framework for hospital diagnostic excellence.

fact_summary: Referral triage creates a new work queue where responsibility can be unclear to patient and referrer.
fact_context: Stage: referral; specialist review; follow-up; action: Referral Assessment Services allow provider review before booking; outcomes include booking, redirecting, requesting more info, or returning advice.
topic_category: wrong specialty; referral delay; poor handoff
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Digital pathway details from NHS e-RS documentation.

fact_summary: Straight-to-test pathways are efficient only when referral information is complete.
fact_context: Stage: referral; imaging ordered; labs ordered; specialist review; action: Diagnostic e-RS models include direct-to-test, clinical assessment then test, and one-stop consultant-led models.
topic_category: incomplete history; wrong specialty; documentation error
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Operational source on outpatient diagnostics.

fact_summary: Advice can reduce unnecessary appointments but must not become a soft rejection of unresolved symptoms.
fact_context: Stage: referral; specialist review; imaging/labs ordered; follow-up; action: Improvement guide promotes advice/guidance, straight-to-test, pre-appointment hubs; one GI case cut waits through advice and e-RS.
topic_category: referral delay; no follow-up ownership; normal-test trap
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Includes clinician voice and pathway redesign example.

fact_summary: Safety-net clinics may be responsible for follow-up despite care occurring elsewhere.
fact_context: Stage: referral; discharge; follow-up; action: Health centers must have referral/hospital arrangements and processes to receive/record ED or hospital visit information and follow-up actions.
topic_category: poor handoff; fragmented care; no follow-up ownership
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Federal compliance source for FQHC workflow expectations.

fact_summary: Coordination is a model, not a closed-loop diagnostic guarantee.
fact_context: Stage: long-term monitoring; follow-up; referral; action: PCMH recognition emphasizes care coordination and continuous quality improvement in health centers.
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Useful institutional context for community health centers.

fact_summary: Abnormal screening is only useful if diagnostic colonoscopy happens.
fact_context: Stage: public health screening; lab interpretation; referral; follow-up; action: Mailed FIT outreach identified abnormal results; centralized patient navigation addressed informational, emotional, financial and transport barriers to colonoscopy.
setting_or_context: community health center / public health screening
topic_category: abnormal result not followed up; patient self-advocacy required; no follow-up ownership
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Implementation study in FQHC safety-net setting; strong fit to product workflows.

fact_summary: Navigation fails if it is not staffed, documented, partnered and tracked.
fact_context: Stage: public health screening; referral; follow-up; action: PRECISE trial implementation support trained navigators and documented challenges in workload, documentation, partnerships and tracking.
topic_category: referral delay; fragmented care; documentation error
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Recent implementation lessons for community health settings.

fact_summary: Screening can create false reassurance if symptoms are separated from screening result interpretation.
fact_context: Stage: before visit; public health screening; lab interpretation; referral; follow-up; action: Eligible people receive FIT at home; results usually within 2 weeks; further tests lead to specialist nurse and usually colonoscopy; negative screen does not rule out cancer if symptoms occur.
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Patient-facing pathway; strong flow map source.

fact_summary: Population screening has a built-in non-attendance black zone.
fact_context: Stage: before visit; public health screening; imaging ordered; imaging interpretation; specialist review; action: Programme sends invitations, performs mammography, recalls non-attenders/technical failures, and sends abnormal cases to assessment clinic/MDT.
topic_category: no follow-up ownership; abnormal result not followed up; patient self-advocacy required
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: National screening pathway source.

fact_summary: The problem is often not clinical detection but completion of the next step.
fact_context: Stage: before visit; referral; follow-up; action: CDC describes navigators helping people overcome practical, informational and trust barriers to screening and diagnosis.
topic_category: fragmented care; patient self-advocacy required; referral delay
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Public health source for patient navigation design.

fact_summary: Community screening creates many positive signals that need tracking beyond the first contact.
fact_context: Stage: before visit; home/community visit; public health screening; referral; long-term monitoring; action: HWCs use empanelment, home/community interaction and universal screening to identify risks before clinic attendance; CHO leads HWC team and referrals.
topic_category: referral delay; no follow-up ownership; lab trend missed
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Government operational guidelines; rich low-resource workflow source.

fact_summary: Low-resource workflows depend heavily on algorithms and referral availability.
fact_context: Stage: medical history; physical exam; referral; emergency escalation; action: IMCI uses evidence-based algorithms based on history/signs/symptoms when lab/radiology support is limited; includes treatment, counselling and referral for severe illness.
topic_category: red flag missed; referral delay; incomplete history
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: WHO source for low-resource diagnostic flow.

fact_summary: The first diagnostic encounter may happen at home, not a clinic.
fact_context: Stage: before visit; home/community visit; emergency escalation; action: iCCM trains/supplies CHWs to diagnose/classify/treat uncomplicated cases and refer severe illness.
topic_category: referral delay; poor handoff; low-resource supply gap
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Global child-health programme documentation.

fact_summary: Community programmes can generate care access but require supervision and integration with facilities.
fact_context: Stage: home/community visit; referral; follow-up; action: RAcE supported iCCM scale-up through CHW training, supplies and diagnosis/treatment/referral in five countries.
topic_category: poor handoff; no follow-up ownership; fragmented care
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Programme-scale low-resource evidence.

fact_summary: In many rural OPDs, primary and emergency care share a front door with no formal triage.
fact_context: Stage: arrival; nurse triage; vitals; first clinician assessment; action: Rural OPD implemented IITT-adapted structured triage using a three-colour checklist on charts and short training; patient flow improved without added resources.
topic_category: red flag missed; arrival; poor handoff
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Real-world QI study in rural Uganda.

fact_summary: Community knowledge is powerful but can be under-documented or poorly integrated into clinical records.
fact_context: Stage: home/community visit; long-term monitoring; referral; action: CHWs provide home basic care, register families, relay information to teams, resolve low-level problems and refer complex problems.
topic_category: fragmented care; no one connected the dots; documentation error
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Health-system case study; strong real-world community flow.

fact_summary: Screening must connect to protocolized treatment and follow-up, not just identify high BP.
fact_context: Stage: vitals; labs ordered; lab interpretation; long-term monitoring; action: PAHO HEARTS clinical pathways standardize treatment protocols, proper BP measurement and cardiovascular risk assessment in PHC.
topic_category: lab/vital trend missed; no follow-up ownership
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Regional implementation source, not a single clinic case.

fact_summary: Preventive flow identifies risk before symptoms but depends on participation and sustained behaviour support.
fact_context: Stage: before visit; public health screening; long-term monitoring; action: Specific health checkups screen insured people/dependents age 40+; results stratify into information provision, motivational support or proactive support.
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Insurer programme source; East Asia preventive flow.

fact_summary: Screening result delivery by mail does not ensure comprehension or completion of confirmation testing.
fact_context: Stage: before visit; public health screening; lab interpretation; follow-up; action: Patient checks eligibility, visits authorized hospital with ID/checkup card, receives results by mail; suspected disease may receive confirmation checkup.
topic_category: abnormal result not followed up; no follow-up ownership
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Official national screening flow source.

fact_summary: Countries need a route for serious nonspecific symptoms, not only organ-specific red flags.
fact_context: Stage: medical history; labs ordered; referral; specialist review; action: Three-legged strategy: urgent alarm symptoms to organ-specific pathways; serious nonspecific symptoms to tests and diagnostic centres; vague low-risk symptoms managed by GP with diagnostic access.
topic_category: rare disease delay; referral delay; no one connected the dots
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Health-system case study of a distinctive diagnostic pathway model.

fact_summary: Nonspecific symptoms can represent high-yield diagnostic risk when systematically routed.
fact_context: Stage: referral; specialist review; labs/imaging ordered; action: Cohort study of urgent referral to Denmark's non-organ-specific cancer pathway; around one fifth had cancer.
topic_category: rare disease delay; referral delay; normal-test trap
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Peer-reviewed evidence for nonspecific symptom diagnostic pathways.

fact_summary: Digitized history reduces fragmentation but still requires pattern recognition and alert design.
fact_context: Stage: before visit; first clinician assessment; long-term monitoring; action: Israel's HMO-based system begins with family doctor/community health centers and uses longstanding digital records/HIE to give clinicians historical data at point of care.
topic_category: lab trend missed; no one connected the dots
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
notes: Health-system overview; not a single clinical workflow manual.

fact_summary: Result delivery is not the same as result action.
fact_context: Stage: labs ordered; imaging ordered; lab interpretation; follow-up; action: Diagnostic services are computerized from referral through collection/test and result delivery to physician and user stations; teleradiology centralizes interpretation for distant sites.
topic_category: abnormal result not followed up; no follow-up ownership
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues

fact_summary: Diagnostic failure is often an operations/communication problem, not just cognitive error.
